mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, tight labor market conditions trigger wage inflation expectations that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5 percent, contradicting the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: The joint divergence between industrial production growth and residential housing starts signals a supply-demand imbalance that forces equity risk premiums higher. When INDPRO rises while HOUST falls, forward equity returns decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day forward returns across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating industrial output, supply chain bottlenecks amplify price stickiness, causing real GDP growth to decouple from equity valuations. Sustained INDPRO gains over a quarter compress forward S&P 500 returns as margin pressures materialize.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positively correlated with INDPRO growth rates in more than 70 percent of rolling 10-year windows, invalidating the bottleneck margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive policy tightening that shocks equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb rate hikes exceeding 0.25pp in 3 months without generating negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by RSAFS, directly drive corporate revenue growth expectations. A positive quarter-over-quarter change in RSAFS mechanically lifts forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show zero correlation with RSAFS growth rates across a 15-year out-of-sample test, rejecting the revenue growth transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and contraction in real savings rates indicates a consumption-driven economy operating beyond sustainable capacity. This dual signal forces valuation multiple compression, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and RSAFS signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered episodes across two decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, CPIAUCSL acceleration erodes real yields and forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over market stability. This regime shifts the discount rate premium upward, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL accelerates alongside rising FEDFUNDS, disproving the inflation-driven multiple compression hypothesis.
